title: Elementary
description: Build on your basics — talk about daily life, health, work, weather, and your past

units.title: Daily Routines
units.description: Describe your daily schedule from morning to night

lessons.title: Morning Routine
lessons.description: Waking up, getting ready, and breakfast vocabulary

exercises.sentence: Ik ___ elke ochtend om zeven uur op.
exercises.translation: I get up every morning at seven o'clock.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ik poets mijn tanden voor het ontbijt.
exercises.translation: I brush my teeth before breakfast.

lessons.title: At Work or School
lessons.description: Talking about your working day

exercises.question: How do you say 'lunch break' in Dutch?

exercises.sentence: De ___ begint om negen uur.
exercises.translation: The meeting starts at nine o'clock.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ik werk van negen tot vijf.
exercises.translation: I work from nine to five.

lessons.title: Evening & Hobbies
lessons.description: Free time, hobbies, and relaxing

exercises.sentence: In de avond ___ ik graag een boek.
exercises.translation: In the evening I like to read a book.
My hobby is playing guitar.

lessons.title: The Weekend
lessons.description: Weekend plans and activities

exercises.sentence: In het weekend ___ ik graag uit.
exercises.translation: At the weekend I like to sleep in.

exercises.correctAnswer: Zaterdag ga ik naar de markt.
exercises.translation: On Saturday I go to the market.

lessons.title: Frequency Adverbs
lessons.description: Always, sometimes, never — how often do you do things?

exercises.sentence: Ik ga ___ naar de sportschool.
exercises.translation: I never go to the gym.
She always eats healthily.

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review Unit 1 content

exercises.sentence: Ik ___ elke dag om acht uur op.
exercises.translation: I get up every day at eight o'clock.
He always works hard.

exercises.sentence: In het weekend ___ ik graag.
exercises.translation: At the weekend I like to relax.

units.title: Health & Body
units.description: Body parts, common ailments, and visiting the doctor

lessons.title: Parts of the Body
lessons.description: Head, shoulders, knees and toes — in Dutch

exercises.sentence: Mijn ___ doet pijn.
exercises.translation: My back hurts.

exercises.correctAnswer: Hij heeft pijn in zijn been.
exercises.translation: He has pain in his leg.

lessons.title: Feeling Ill
lessons.description: Describing symptoms: headache, fever, stomach ache

exercises.sentence: Ik heb ___ en ik moet naar de dokter.
exercises.translation: I have a fever and I need to go to the doctor.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ik voel me niet lekker vandaag.
exercises.translation: I do not feel well today.

lessons.title: At the Doctor
lessons.description: Making an appointment and explaining symptoms

exercises.sentence: Ik wil graag een ___ maken bij de dokter.
exercises.translation: I would like to make an appointment with the doctor.

exercises.correctAnswer: De dokter schrijft een recept voor.
exercises.translation: The doctor writes a prescription.

lessons.title: At the Pharmacy
lessons.description: Buying medicine and understanding instructions

exercises.sentence: Neem twee ___ drie keer per dag.
exercises.translation: Take two tablets three times a day.
Do you have something for a headache?

lessons.title: Healthy Living
lessons.description: Sport, diet, sleep — healthy lifestyle vocabulary

exercises.sentence: Het is belangrijk om elke dag te ___.
exercises.translation: It is important to exercise every day.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ze eet elke dag veel groenten.
exercises.translation: She eats a lot of vegetables every day.

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review Unit 2 content

exercises.sentence: Ik heb ___ in mijn rug.
exercises.translation: I have pain in my back.

exercises.correctAnswer: De apotheek is om de hoek.
exercises.translation: The pharmacy is around the corner.

exercises.sentence: Ik ga elke week naar de ___.
exercises.translation: I go to the gym every week.

units.title: Housing & Neighbourhood
units.description: Talk about where you live, your home, and your neighbourhood in the Netherlands

lessons.title: Types of Housing
lessons.description: Apartments, houses, and student rooms in the Netherlands

exercises.sentence: Ik woon in een ___ op de derde verdieping.
exercises.translation: I live in an apartment on the third floor.
In the Netherlands there are many terraced houses.

lessons.title: Rooms & Furniture
lessons.description: Describe the rooms and furniture in your home

exercises.sentence: De ___ staat in de slaapkamer.
exercises.translation: The wardrobe is in the bedroom.

exercises.correctAnswer: Onze woonkamer heeft een grote bank.
exercises.translation: Our living room has a big sofa.

lessons.title: Your Neighbourhood
lessons.description: Shops, parks, and schools near your home

exercises.sentence: Er is een ___ vlak bij mijn huis.
exercises.translation: There is a supermarket right near my house.

exercises.correctAnswer: De school is aan het einde van de straat.
exercises.translation: The school is at the end of the street.

lessons.title: Renting in the Netherlands
lessons.description: Renting vocabulary and talking to your landlord

exercises.sentence: De ___ is vijfhonderd euro per maand.
exercises.translation: The rent is five hundred euros per month.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ik wil graag het huurcontract tekenen.
exercises.translation: I would like to sign the rental contract.

exercises.question: What does 'opleveren' mean in a rental context?

lessons.title: Dutch Cities & Geography
lessons.description: Major cities, the Randstad, and Dutch provinces

exercises.question: What is the capital of the Netherlands?

exercises.sentence: Nederland heeft twaalf ___.
exercises.translation: The Netherlands has twelve provinces.

exercises.correctAnswer: Amsterdam is de hoofdstad van Nederland.
exercises.translation: Amsterdam is the capital of the Netherlands.

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review Unit 3 content

exercises.sentence: Ik woon in een groot ___ in Amsterdam.
exercises.translation: I live in a large apartment in Amsterdam.

exercises.correctAnswer: De bibliotheek is vlak bij het park.
exercises.translation: The library is right next to the park.

exercises.question: What is the Dutch word for 'deposit' (in a rental)?

exercises.sentence: Nederland heeft twaalf ___.
exercises.translation: The Netherlands has twelve provinces.

units.title: Work & Study
units.description: Talk about your job, education, and professional life in Dutch

lessons.title: Jobs & Professions
lessons.description: Common job titles and what they involve

exercises.sentence: Mijn vader werkt als ___.
exercises.translation: My father works as an engineer.

exercises.correctAnswer: Zij is verpleegkundige in het ziekenhuis.
exercises.translation: She is a nurse in the hospital.

lessons.title: Education System
lessons.description: Dutch school types, university, and studying

exercises.sentence: Ik ___ psychologie aan de universiteit.
exercises.translation: I study psychology at university.

exercises.correctAnswer: In Nederland begint school om half negen.
exercises.translation: In the Netherlands school starts at half past eight.

lessons.title: At the Office
lessons.description: Office vocabulary, meetings, and working with colleagues

exercises.sentence: De ___ is morgen om twaalf uur.
exercises.translation: The deadline is tomorrow at twelve o'clock.

exercises.correctAnswer: Wij hebben elke maandag een vergadering.
exercises.translation: We have a meeting every Monday.

lessons.title: Work-Life Balance
lessons.description: Working hours, holidays, and part-time culture in the Netherlands

exercises.sentence: Ik werk drie dagen per week, want ik werk ___.
exercises.translation: I work three days a week because I work part time.

exercises.correctAnswer: In Nederland werken veel mensen parttime.
exercises.translation: In the Netherlands many people work part time.

lessons.title: Applying for a Job
lessons.description: CV vocabulary, cover letters, and interview phrases

exercises.question: What does 'cv' stand for in Dutch?

exercises.sentence: Ik heb mijn ___ en motivatiebrief opgestuurd.
exercises.translation: I have sent my CV and cover letter.

exercises.correctAnswer: Het sollicitatiegesprek is volgende week dinsdag.
exercises.translation: The job interview is next Tuesday.

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review Unit 4 content

exercises.sentence: Ze heeft veel ___ in de zorgsector.
exercises.translation: She has a lot of work experience in the healthcare sector.

exercises.correctAnswer: Hij solliciteert naar een nieuwe baan.
exercises.translation: He is applying for a new job.

exercises.sentence: In Nederland werken veel mensen ___.
exercises.translation: In the Netherlands many people work part time.

units.title: Weather & Seasons
units.description: Describe the weather, the four seasons, and the famously wet Dutch climate

lessons.title: Weather Vocabulary
lessons.description: Sun, rain, wind, clouds — essential Dutch weather words

exercises.sentence: Het is vandaag ___ en koud.
exercises.translation: It is cloudy and cold today.

exercises.correctAnswer: Het regent bijna elke dag in Nederland.
exercises.translation: It rains almost every day in the Netherlands.

lessons.title: The Four Seasons
lessons.description: Lente, zomer, herfst, winter — and seasonal activities

exercises.sentence: In de ___ bloeien de tulpen.
exercises.translation: In spring the tulips bloom.

exercises.correctAnswer: Mijn favoriete seizoen is de zomer.
exercises.translation: My favourite season is summer.

exercises.question: In which season do leaves fall from trees in the Netherlands?

lessons.title: Weather Forecasts
lessons.description: Reading and listening to Dutch weather forecasts

exercises.question: What does 'graad' mean in weather context?

exercises.sentence: Morgen worden er ___ verwacht.
exercises.translation: Tomorrow showers are expected.

exercises.correctAnswer: De temperatuur stijgt tot twintig graden.
exercises.translation: The temperature rises to twenty degrees.

lessons.title: Dutch Climate
lessons.description: Why it always rains and how the Dutch cope with it

exercises.sentence: In Nederland regent het vaak, dus ik neem altijd een ___ mee.
exercises.translation: In the Netherlands it often rains, so I always bring an umbrella.

exercises.correctAnswer: Het Nederlandse klimaat is nat en gematigd.
exercises.translation: The Dutch climate is wet and temperate.

lessons.title: Dressing for the Weather
lessons.description: Clothing vocabulary in seasonal context

exercises.question: What do you wear on your hands when it is cold?

exercises.sentence: Het is koud buiten, dus trek een ___ aan.
exercises.translation: It is cold outside, so put on a coat.

exercises.correctAnswer: In de winter draag ik een warme jas.
exercises.translation: In winter I wear a warm coat.

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review Unit 5 content

exercises.sentence: In de ___ vallen de bladeren van de bomen.
exercises.translation: In autumn the leaves fall from the trees.

exercises.correctAnswer: Neem een paraplu mee, want het gaat regenen.
exercises.translation: Take an umbrella because it is going to rain.

exercises.sentence: In de winter draag ik een warme ___.
exercises.translation: In winter I wear a warm coat.

units.title: Past Tense
units.description: Talk about what happened yesterday, last week, and in the past

lessons.title: Regular Past Tense
lessons.description: Forming past tense verbs with -te and -de endings

exercises.question: What is the past tense of 'werken'?
What is the past tense of 'leven'?

exercises.sentence: Gisteren ___ ik de hele dag.
exercises.translation: Yesterday I worked all day.
She played guitar yesterday.

exercises.question: Verbs ending in 't' or 'k' in the stem use which past tense ending?

lessons.title: Perfect Tense with hebben
lessons.description: Ik heb gewerkt — past participles with hebben

exercises.question: What is the past participle of 'werken'?
Which sentence uses the perfect tense correctly?

exercises.sentence: Ik heb een boek ___.
exercises.translation: I have read a book.

exercises.correctAnswer: Hij heeft de hele dag geslapen.
exercises.translation: He has slept the whole day.

exercises.question: How do you form the past participle of a regular verb?

lessons.title: Perfect Tense with zijn
lessons.description: Ik ben gegaan — past participles with zijn for motion verbs

exercises.question: Which auxiliary verb does 'gaan' use in the perfect tense?
Which sentence is correct?
options: Ik heb naar huis gegaan zijn

exercises.sentence: Wij zijn gisteren naar Amsterdam ___.
exercises.translation: We went to Amsterdam yesterday.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ze is vroeg naar bed gegaan.
exercises.translation: She went to bed early.

exercises.question: Motion verbs and change-of-state verbs typically use which auxiliary?

lessons.title: Common Irregular Verbs
lessons.description: gaan→gegaan, komen→gekomen, zien→gezien and more

exercises.question: What is the past participle of 'zien'?
What is the past participle of 'schrijven'?

exercises.sentence: Heb je de film al ___?
exercises.translation: Have you seen the film yet?
I spoke to him yesterday.

lessons.title: Telling Stories in the Past
lessons.description: Narrating past events using all past tense forms

exercises.sentence: ___ ben ik naar het museum geweest.
exercises.translation: Yesterday I went to the museum.

exercises.correctAnswer: Vorige week hebben we een feestje gevierd.
exercises.translation: Last week we celebrated a party.

exercises.question: Which time expression refers to the past?

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.description: Review Unit 6 content

exercises.question: What is the past participle of 'gaan'?

exercises.sentence: Hij ___ gisteren zijn huiswerk.
exercises.translation: He did his homework yesterday.

exercises.correctAnswer: Ik ben vorige week naar Parijs geweest.
exercises.translation: I was in Paris last week.

exercises.question: Which auxiliary is used with 'komen' in the perfect tense?

exercises.sentence: Heb jij die film al ___?
exercises.translation: Have you seen that film yet?
